channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Andy Burnham pledges end to political instability as he becomes UK PM
item.description: Britain's seventh PM in a decade pledged major changes in bid to see off populist forces that are making large gains.

item.title: What are ‘civilian’ sites in international law and where have US, Iran hit?
item.description: The US has escalated attacks on Iranian roads, bridges and power plants, and Iran too, has responded.

item.title: What we know about US soldiers killed in Iran war, and Iranian casualties
item.description: Trump has expressed sadness and vowed to hit Iran 'very hard' to honour killed US soldiers.

item.title: Petro to give farewell speech amid Colombia transfer-of-power tensions
item.description: The outgoing president has called for protests as he continues to dispute the results of last month's election.

item.title: Israeli ministers and settlers call for return to Gaza settlements
item.description: ‘Gaza is ours.’

item.title: Burnham’s move to scrap UK’s digital ID plans earns cheers and criticism
item.description: Digital inclusion and civil liberties campaigners welcome the U-turn — for different reasons. But some are unhappy.

item.title: Why Palestinians in Gaza are celebrating Spain’s World Cup win
item.description: Thousands gathered across Gaza to watch the World Cup final, with many backing Spain over its support for Palestinians.

item.title: Who is Khalil al-Hayya, Hamas’s newly elected political chief?
item.description: Veteran negotiator assumes Palestinian group's overall leadership following a wartime election process.

item.title: Since Islamabad MoU: What’s changed for the US, Iran and the Gulf?
item.description: We explain how the war has changed for all the parties since their MoU unravelled and as the conflict has escalated.

item.title: ‘I want to be honest with you’ says new UK PM Andy Burnham
item.description: ‘I want to be honest with you’ says new UK PM Andy Burnham

item.title: Russian strike on ship near Odesa kills 10, Ukraine hits Moscow with drones
item.description: Ukraine launches hundreds of drones towards Moscow a day after Russia unleashed a massive barrage of ballistic missiles.

item.title: India’s Rohit Sharma ends retirement talk as World Cup 2027 still in sight
item.description: Rohit struck 138 off 110 balls in defeat by England in third ODI, which some speculated could be his last for India.

item.title: Argentina fans throw stones at police after World Cup defeat
item.description: Argentina fans throw stones at police after World Cup defeat

item.title: Don’t believe Andy Burnham’s PR machine
item.description: As his empty Gaza apology clearly demonstrates, he will not bring the radical change Labour really needs.

item.title: Why is Christopher Nolan’s Odyssey so controversial?
item.description: Controversy has centred largely on casting, but it also reflects broader debates on who gets to retell this epic story.

item.title: Iran says Spain World Cup win brings joy to critics of US, Israel
item.description: Iran praises Spain's win over Argentina after Spanish government's condemnation of policies on Gaza and war on Iran.

item.title: Why is Iran bombing Gulf states while avoiding US naval assets and Israel?
item.description: Analysts say Iran is using Gulf states to pressure Washington while avoiding direct confrontation with US forces.

item.title: Photos: Police fire tear gas at India’s Cockroach movement supporters
item.description: The youth-led Cockroach Janta Party protests in New Delhi, demanding India's education minister resign over exam leaks.

item.title: ‘I go with a smile’ says outgoing UK PM Keir Starmer in final speech
item.description: ‘I go with a smile’ says outgoing UK PM Keir Starmer in final speech

item.title: Hamas names Khalil al-Hayya as new leader after run-off with Khaled Meshaal
item.description: Al-Hayya was previously one of the five-member leadership council after previous Hamas chief Yahya Sinwar was killed.

item.title: Jubilation in Spain as La Roja beat Argentina to win second World Cup title
item.description: Fans in Spanish city on Mediterranean cheer on both sides as Spain defeat Argentina 1-0 in extra time.

item.title: World Cup: Spain to be welcomed home by million-strong Madrid crowd
item.description: Spain beat Argentina 1-0 in Sunday's final in the US, and are expected to parade the trophy in Madrid on Monday.

item.title: Occupied West Bank World Cup screening halted for funeral
item.description: A screening of the FIFA World Cup final in Ramallah was halted following news that two Palestinians were killed.

item.title: What does Iran’s attack on Kuwait infrastructure mean for Gulf?
item.description: What does Iran’s attack on Kuwait infrastructure mean for Gulf?

item.title: World Cup: Spanish boy, 13, killed in fountain collapse during celebrations
item.description: Tragedy struck in the Salamanca province when the 13-year-old died during celebrations for the win against Argentina.
